shape_decode: cmp,vp,vpc,naive = _,W,_,L : value_profile RESOLVES, naive BLOCKS, cmplog & value_profile_cmplog non-decisive. Pure VP-PRO (CMP_MAP gradient) family — VP's distance-bucket feedback is necessary and sufficient; I2S substitution is neither (cmplog non-decisive).
label_source: Layer-1 resolve pattern (value_profile resolves / naive blocks). The tests below are the INDEPENDENT verification (G2): they measure what the VP-resolving vs naive-blocking CORPUS REACHES (value-distance / structure), never the resolve pattern itself.

hypotheses.label: VP CMP_MAP climbs a monotone distance gradient toward a FIXED target value (single literal / switch constant / masked-equality / terminal byte of a chain); VP-resolving seeds reach a smaller residual distance to that target than naive-blocking seeds, which stall at a fixed distance.

metric_definitions.vp_residual_distance: median over value_profile-RESOLVING seeds of the minimum operand-distance (Hamming over the operand_width_bytes window, or |masked_value - target| for masked-equality gates) between the seed's gate-offset bytes and the gate's target literal/switch constant.
metric_definitions.distance_close_lift: naive_residual_distance - vp_residual_distance (positive => VP seeds sit closer to the target than naive seeds).

libxml2_6655.sense: match (terminal byte of DTD prefix chain)

params.note_mismatch_sense: For the two bloaty mismatch gates the target literal IS the magic the seeds must AVOID; the gradient is the same direction (VP keeps seeds whose distance-from-magic is LARGE while naive stays trapped at distance 0). Arbiter inverts the sign for sense=='mismatch': distance_close_lift = vp_residual_distance - naive_residual_distance (VP FARTHER from magic).

hypotheses.prediction: G1: VP-resolving seeds sit measurably closer to the gate target (or, for mismatch gates, measurably farther from the avoided magic) than naive-blocking seeds — i.e. the corpus carries a value-distance gradient VP exploited. An alternative (naive simply never reached the gate region at all, no gradient) predicts NO distance separation: both seed sets would be uniformly far / random with respect to the operand. A pure structural-assembly mechanism (vp_structural_assembly_gradient) predicts vp~naive on fixed-operand distance but a size/token gap instead. This rules out 'no-gradient navigation gap' and 'structural-only' alternatives.
hypotheses.rule: distance_close_lift > 0 AND vp_residual_distance < naive_residual_distance (after sense-inversion for mismatch gates) AND abs(distance_close_lift) >= 0.25 * naive_residual_distance

hypotheses.label: VP CMP_MAP closes DERIVED integer comparisons (table offsets/lengths computed from structural fields), not a fixed input literal; VP-resolving seeds carry a larger/more-valid parsed structure built from padding, with no fixed-operand distance signal.

measurement.reuse_note: Reuses the SIZE metric of bench/tools/joint_necessity.py (struct_size_and_token_count) but on the vp-RESOLVING vs naive-BLOCKING seed sets (not vpc vs cmplog); orchestrator may parameterize joint_necessity rather than build new code.

metric_definitions.vp_struct_size: median non-padding (non-0x20) structured byte span / valid sub-table field count in value_profile-RESOLVING seeds at the kern-table region.
metric_definitions.vp_residual_distance/naive_residual_dist: fixed-operand distance from H1's metric, computed here ONLY to confirm it does NOT separate (the discriminator vs H1).

hypotheses.prediction: G1: VP-resolving seeds show a structural-size/validity lift over naive-blocking seeds (struct_size_lift > 1) WHILE showing NO fixed-operand distance separation (it is a derived field, not an input literal — data_realities forbid byte-alignment here). The direct-literal alternative (H1) predicts the opposite: a fixed-operand distance gap and NO structural-size gap. This separates derived-structural assembly from direct-literal climbing.

hypotheses.label: VP CMP_MAP steers AWAY from a dominant magic-value basin: there is no single target literal — the true side wants ANY value but the corpus-dominant magic. VP-resolving seeds DEPART the basin (multi-modal: spread over several non-magic format magics), while naive-blocking seeds are pinned at the basin value.

metric_definitions.naive_basin_lock_fraction: fraction of naive-BLOCKING seeds whose 4-byte prefix == the corpus-dominant magic (00 61 73 6d).
metric_definitions.vp_value_modality: count of DISTINCT non-magic 4-byte prefix values across the vp-resolving seed set (multi-modality test — a single-target gradient would be ~uni-modal at one constant; basin escape is multi-modal).
metric_definitions.basin_departure_lift: naive_basin_lock_fraction - vp_basin_lock_fraction (positive => VP escaped the basin that pinned naive).

hypotheses.prediction: G1: naive-blocking seeds are basin-locked (high naive_basin_lock_fraction) while VP-resolving seeds have left the basin (low vp_basin_lock_fraction) AND spread over MULTIPLE distinct non-magic prefixes (vp_value_modality >= 3). The direct-literal alternative (H1) predicts the opposite signature: VP seeds CONVERGING on ONE target value (low modality, ~uni-modal). This is the decisive separator from a fixed-target gradient: basin escape is divergence-AWAY-from-one-value (multi-modal), literal gradient is convergence-TOWARD-one-value (uni-modal).

decision_order_rationale: Try the two SPECIFIC mechanisms first (basin_escape needs a multi-modal departure signature; structural_assembly needs a struct-size lift WITHOUT operand-distance), then fall to the GENERAL direct_value_gradient. This ordering prevents the broad direct-literal rule from absorbing a branch that is really a basin-escape (VP seeds look 'far from magic' under a naive distance metric) or a structural assembly (no fixed literal at all). The arbiter assigns each branch to the FIRST hypothesis whose rule fires.
data_realities_respected: On-disk corpora exist ONLY for curl, harfbuzz, openthread, sqlite3. Of the 12 branches, only harfbuzz_5708_h0, harfbuzz_6006, harfbuzz_6061_h1 (harfbuzz) and sqlite3_14793 (sqlite3) have corpus seeds for value_distance_reached. The bloaty (3), lcms (2), libxml2 (2) branches have NO on-disk corpus and can be scored ONLY if resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds are present in db/blockers.sqlite (the 286 reps have them). Branches with neither => fallback: inconclusive.

VP (CMP_MAP) is a FEEDBACK, not a mutator — no seed_lineage 'gradient event' is measurable. All three tests read the RESOLVING vs BLOCKING seed SETS (what the corpus reached), never a lineage event. Compliant.
I2S is untagged in mutation_op — irrelevant here (cmplog is non-decisive in this shape), so no I2S-event descriptor is proposed.

Structural-assembly gate (harfbuzz_5708_h0) has different-SIZED winning vs blocking seeds, so byte-alignment (byte_freq_ratio / fixed-operand distance) is FORBIDDEN there — H2 uses struct_size_ratio, NOT operand distance, as its positive signal (operand distance is computed only as a negative control).

notes.discovery: 12 branches, all pure VP-PRO CMP_MAP gradient. mechanism_summary describes one underlying technique (distance-bucket feedback VP has and naive lacks) in 4 surface forms: (a) fixed-literal/switch-constant climbing (8: both bloaty mismatch gates, both lcms FOURCC, libxml2 masked surrogate, both harfbuzz codepoint switches, sqlite3 token class), (b) chained byte ratchet (1: libxml2_6655), (c) derived structural assembly (1: harfbuzz_5708_h0), (d) basin escape (1: bloaty_11).
notes.collapse: Collapsed the 4 prior subtypes to 3 discriminable hypotheses. MERGED chained_byte_gradient (libxml2_6655) INTO vp_direct_value_gradient: a multi-step prefix chain that culminates in ONE terminal-byte equality produces the SAME measurable signal as a one-step literal gate — VP-resolving seeds close the residual distance to the terminal operand while naive stalls. Real seed-set data cannot tell 'reached the terminal byte via a 1-step gate' from 'via an N-step chain'; only the terminal residual distance is observable. Keeping them apart would be a distinction the data cannot support. KEPT basin_escape and structural_assembly separate because each has a DISTINCT, oppositely-signed signature: basin_escape is multi-modal DIVERGENCE away from one value (uni-modal convergence would refute it), structural_assembly is a struct-size lift WITH NO fixed-operand distance (the exact inverse of H1's signature). The decision_order + the negative-control terms in each rule make the three mutually exclusive at arbitration.
notes.new_descriptors: Introduces value_distance_reached (registry_tool null) as the family's primary measurement — VP-resolving seeds close operand-distance to the gate target, naive stalls. Two flavors: gate_target_literal (H1, fixed target) and corpus_dominant_basin (H3, distance FROM a dominant value, multi-modal). H2 reuses the size metric of joint_necessity (struct_size_and_token_count) but applied to vp-resolving vs naive-blocking sets — orchestrator may parameterize the existing tool rather than build new code.
notes.g2_independence: All measurements read what the RESOLVING vs BLOCKING corpus REACHED (operand distance, structure size, basin modality) — never the resolve/block pattern that defined the shape. We do not 'verify VP-resolves with VP-resolves': the discriminating signal is a property of the seed BYTES relative to the gate, available independent of which fuzzer ran.
notes.feasibility_caveat: Corpus availability is the binding constraint: 4 of 12 branches (3 harfbuzz + 1 sqlite3) have on-disk corpora outright; the rest depend on resolving/blocking seeds being in db/blockers.sqlite. Branches lacking seed sets fall to inconclusive rather than being labeled from the resolve pattern alone — an honest no-evidence beats an unverified label.
notes.unbuilt_tool: value_distance_reached (descriptor compute key already reserved in tool_registry.json descriptor_vocabulary, but no tool implements it yet) — orchestrator must build/register it before the arbiter can score H1 and H3.
